name: Shia Islam
description: The second-largest branch of Islam, comprising 10-15% of Muslims. Distinguishes itself through reverence for the Ahl al-Bayt (Prophet's family), the Imamate, and distinct jurisprudence. Dominant in Iran and Iraq, with significant minorities in Lebanon, Bahrain, Yemen, and Pakistan. Associated with strong clerical authority and resistance movements.

interReligiousTensions.historicalBasis: Succession dispute, proxy wars across Middle East
